name: Upside-Down Chocolate Cupcakes
recipeInstructions:
Makes 12 cupcakes
Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Well grease 12 muffin pan cups.
In a small saucepan, combine the 1/4 cup melted butter, brown sugar, and 1 tbsp water. Simmer for 1 minute. Place 3 or 4 walnut halves in each of the prepared muffin pan cups. Spoon the cooked mixture over the walnuts.
Sift flour, cocoa, baking soda, and salt together; set aside. Cream the 1/4 cup butter until smooth. Gradually add sugar and continue creaming until light and fluffy. Beat in egg and vanilla. Blend in dry ingredients alternately with the 1/2 cup water, beginning and ending with dry ingredients.
Fill muffin pan cups about half full with batter. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes, until the surface springs back when gently touched with a fingertip. Remove from the muffin pan. Let cool inverted on wire racks.
In a small saucepan, combine milk and chocolate chips over very low flame, stirring constantly, until smooth and thickened about 10 minutes. Stir in 1 tbsp butter; keep warm. Spread on sides of cupcakes.
(Note: This recipe is based on information from Whitman's Chocolate Cookbook, ISBN 0-517-64157-7.)
